description: Idea-exploration workflow — research the domain, sketch a tentative plan, adversarially critique it, research again to verify which critiques are real, then exit at a decision checkpoint. Use to determine whether to build an idea. No implementation phases — when you decide yes, run /forgewright:workflow-run greenfield (or feature) to actually build it.

phases.name: verify-critique
phases.instruction: Invoke the "agentwright:research" skill via the Skill tool. This phase consumes the "critique" artifact — read artifacts/critique.md before invoking the skill. The research target is: for each critique point, investigate whether the critique's underlying claim holds up against current literature, prior art, and real-world evidence. Score each critique as VALID (the concern is substantiated), PARTIAL (some merit but overstated or context-dependent), or INVALID (the critique's premise is wrong). Write the result to artifacts/critique-verification.md as a per-critique table with the verdict and citations, then report the path back via workflow-advance --artifact-path <path>. When done, call workflow-advance --result completed.

phases.name: decision
phases.summary: Idea exploration complete. Artifacts: research.md, plan.md, critique.md, critique-verification.md. User reviews and decides. If you decide to build, run /forgewright:workflow-run greenfield (or feature for an existing codebase). If not, /forgewright:workflow-stop this workflow to mark it cancelled.
